rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YOUNG JOON CHO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) The attorney's signature and petitioner's verification must be removed from the proposed order (only the Court's signature block should be provided). 2) A legal description of the estate real property must be included in the body of the proposed order (no attachment or exhibit is allowed). 3) The probate referee's commission ($1,771) must be paid before the Court can approve final distribution. Please file a copy of a receipt or other proof of payment. 4) An allegation must be made that the petitioner or other beneficiary has advanced sufficient cash (including a refund from the attorney) to the estate for payment of the attorneys' fees and costs, or the estate real property must be distributed in kind, subject to a lien securing the attorneys' fees and costs. See Probate Code section 10831(d) and LRSF 14.56. Each attorney's "retainer" can only be used to pay the costs (but not fees) without prior court approval. The retainer balance, if any, must be refunded to the petitioner so that payment of the attorneys' fees can be ordered to be made out of the estate after the Court approves the attorneys' fees and final distribution. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATILDA YIM ONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/20/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner other than a copy of the form DE-147S. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Notwithstanding the lack of courtesy copies, the papers were reviewed online as a one-time courtesy. (3) The petition caption shows that the will is LOST, but an original will was later lodged. (4) Item 6d of the petition is not the FIRST box that applies if the petitioner is a nephew. (5) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who is/was the decedent's father and when did he die? (6) Item 8 of the petition must clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust. (7) If the petitioner is the trustee, all of the beneficiaries of the trust must also be listed and must receive notice. (8) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures attached to the notice of hearing, not filed separately. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
